content: We can frame interactive compressed multimedia (ICM) training for streaming as an autonomous, goal-driven agent that actively adjusts its processing pipeline to exert power over the end-user’s viewing experience, rather than treating it as a passive sequence of compression and delivery steps. This ICM agent would act as an active efficient cause that shapes its own streaming processing workflow in real time, prioritizing bitrate allocation and frame processing based on both network conditions and the user’s implicit attention patterns in the moment.
source_topics: Streaming experience processing for ICM training
trigger_topic: Streaming experience processing for ICM training
